# retro: af retro mines archived workstream notes under
# $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/archive/<name>/note.md (ADR-058).
#
# Fixtures: two archived notes with af_* YAML frontmatter (alpha tagged
# "infra", beta tagged "ui"), plus one bare archive entry without a
# note.md (skipped silently). A script-local pi fake serves the --ai
# narrative so no real agent is contacted.
#
# Scenarios:
#   1. no archive dir at all — hard error.
#   2. plain listing — one line per note.
#   3. --tag / --search filters narrow the set.
#   4. --ai appends a narrative from the (fake) agent.
#   5. bad --since duration — usage error.
#   6. --ai with zero matching notes — hard error.

# ── Scenario 1: no archive directory ──────────────────────────────────────────
env HOME=$WORK/home-empty
mkdir $WORK/home-empty

! exec af retro
stderr 'archive directory does not exist'

# ── Scenario 2: listing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
env HOME=$WORK/home

# A bare archive entry without note.md predates note archiving; it must
# be skipped without a warning or error.
mkdir $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/archive/bare-entry

exec af retro
stdout '- 2026-05-01 \[alpha\] feat/alpha'
stdout '- 2026-06-01 \[beta\] feat/beta'

# ── Scenario 3: filters ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# --tag matches af_tags (alpha has "infra"; beta does not).
exec af retro --tag infra
stdout 'alpha'
! stdout 'beta'

# --search is a substring match on the note body.
exec af retro --search 'frontend polish'
stdout 'beta'
! stdout 'alpha'

# Filters that match nothing report cleanly (exit 0).
exec af retro --tag nonexistent-tag
stdout 'no archived workstreams matched'

# ── Scenario 4: --ai narrative via the fake agent ─────────────────────────────
chmod 755 mypi/pi
env PATH=$WORK/mypi:$PATH

exec af retro --ai
stdout '## Narrative'
stdout 'FAKE RETRO NARRATIVE'

# ── Scenario 5: invalid --since ───────────────────────────────────────────────
! exec af retro --since bogus
stderr 'parse --since'

# ── Scenario 6: --ai with no matching notes ───────────────────────────────────
! exec af retro --ai --tag nonexistent-tag
stderr 'requires at least one matching note'

# ── Fixtures ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- home/.local/share/af/v1/archive/alpha/note.md --
---
af_schema: 1
af_session: alpha
af_repo: repo
af_branch: feat/alpha
af_base_branch: main
af_status: completed
af_started_at: 2026-05-01T10:00:00Z
af_completed_at: null
af_pr_number: 0
af_pr_url: ""
af_pr_state: ""
tags: [af]
af_tags: [infra]
---
# alpha

## Log

database migration work
-- home/.local/share/af/v1/archive/beta/note.md --
---
af_schema: 1
af_session: beta
af_repo: repo
af_branch: feat/beta
af_base_branch: main
af_status: completed
af_started_at: 2026-06-01T09:00:00Z
af_completed_at: null
af_pr_number: 0
af_pr_url: ""
af_pr_state: ""
tags: [af]
af_tags: [ui]
---
# beta

## Log

frontend polish pass
-- mypi/pi --
#!/bin/sh
# Fake pi agent for af retro --ai: consumes the prompt on stdin and
# prints a canned narrative (BodyCmd argv is `pi --print`).
if [ "$1" = "--print" ]; then
  cat >/dev/null
  echo "FAKE RETRO NARRATIVE"
  exit 0
fi
echo "fake pi $*"
exit 0
